response_raw:
{
"mode": "human",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it's essential to be aware of the potential sources of false information. This includes misinformation campaigns by political groups or individuals seeking to manipulate public opinion. Additionally, bots and other automated accounts can also spread false information. To identify these sources, use fact-checking websites and tools that can help verify the accuracy of posts. Also, be cautious when sharing content, especially if it seems too good (or bad) to be true. Use reputable news sources for your information and be mindful of your own biases. By being aware of these potential sources of fake content and taking steps to verify their accuracy, you can better manage and deal with false information on social media.",

response_parsed.opinion: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it's essential to be aware of the potential sources of false information. This includes misinformation campaigns by political groups or individuals seeking to manipulate public opinion. Additionally, bots and other automated accounts can also spread false information. To identify these sources, use fact-checking websites and tools that can help verify the accuracy of posts. Also, be cautious when sharing content, especially if it seems too good (or bad) to be true. Use reputable news sources for your information and be mindful of your own biases. By being aware of these potential sources of fake content and taking steps to verify their accuracy, you can better manage and deal with false information on social media.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it's crucial to be aware of the potential sources of misinformation. This includes political groups or individuals seeking to manipulate public opinion through misinformation campaigns. Additionally, bots and other automated accounts can also spread false information. To identify these sources, use fact-checking websites and tools that can help verify the accuracy of posts. Moreover, be cautious when sharing content, especially if it seems too good (or bad) to be true. Use reputable news sources for your information and be mindful of your own biases. By being aware of these potential sources of fake content and taking steps to verify their accuracy, you can better manage and deal with false information on social media.
